title: NOT_AN_AGGREGATE: GROUP BY with positional arguments in view on remote node
description:
### Describe what's wrong
Query returns error `is not under aggregate function and not in GROUP BY keys` when querying view containing GROUP BY with positional arguments on remote node
Workaround: change GROUP BY positional arguments to explicit columns
### Does it reproduce on the most recent release?
Yes
### How to reproduce
```sql
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS narrative;
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS experiment_by_platform;
CREATE TABLE narratives
(
`sector` String,
)
ENGINE = MergeTree
ORDER BY sector;
CREATE VIEW experiment_by_platform
(
`sector` String,
)
AS SELECT
sector
FROM narratives
GROUP BY 1; -- replace it with `sector` to avoid the issue
SELECT *
FROM remote('127.0.0.{1,2,3}', currentDatabase(), experiment_by_platform);
```
https://fiddle.clickhouse.com/64c3a1f4-4a83-4c08-bb20-6712b35f574e
### Expected behavior
_No response_
### Error message and/or stacktrace
https://pastila.nl/?000a94ae/4382a2120e20b27d22f7653d0ed9678a#zN9sJa4ER7KEeKc1OtB0LA==
### Additional context
_No response_
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_narratives (id INTEGER, sector TEXT);
CREATE VIEW test_experiment AS SELECT sector FROM test_narratives GROUP BY sector;
key_bug_features: GROUP BY with positional arguments in view on remote node
